core_mechanism: Eigendecomposition of the local Jacobian to split a stiff dynamical system into fast (equilibrated/exhausted) and slow (active) modes and project the dynamics onto the slow invariant manifold, so the reduced slow motion can be advanced with an explicit adaptive step — a spectral multiple-timescale (singular-perturbation / slow-manifold) decomposition.
brief_reason: The abstract states the framework 'exploits a local time-scale decomposition provided by the eigensystem of the Jacobian (the kernel set)' to enable explicit adaptive integration of otherwise-stiff ODEs, which is exactly a spectral fast/slow timescale separation onto a slow manifold (the multi-resolution hash table merely precomputes and caches this eigensystem).
